what_it_does: Defines an additive patch for an existing content-router Supabase edge function: a small if-check (in comments) that intercepts the /oauth/consent path, plus an executable serveConsent function that returns an HTML page rendering an OAuth consent/authorization screen for 'Vi (Claude Design)'.
observations: The file is structured as a patch with explicit insertion instructions in comments: BLOCK 1 (the if-check) is given only as commented text to paste, while BLOCK 2 (serveConsent) is real code.
serveConsent builds a self-contained HTML string and returns it as a text/html Response with Cache-Control: no-store.
The HTML loads @supabase/supabase-js v2 from cdn.jsdelivr.net and creates a browser Supabase client using injected supabaseUrl and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY.
The consent flow reads authorization_id from the query string and calls sb.auth.oauth.approveAuthorization(authId), then redirects to data.redirect_url.
Sign-in supports both email and phone OTP via signInWithOtp / verifyOtp, with tabbed UI to switch between methods.
If a session already exists, it skips login and shows the 'Allow Vi' button directly.
References supabaseUrl as an in-scope variable (not defined in this file), implying it exists in the target content-router module.
Consent copy states Vi wants access to the user's Company 'design' channel and design submissions, labeled as 'Tim's first-party connector.'
standouts: Uses Supabase's auth.oauth.approveAuthorization API to complete a first-party OAuth authorization flow.
Entire consent UI (markup, styles, login logic, OTP verify, approve) is inlined into one server-returned HTML string.
conventions: Follows an explicitly additive-patch convention: insert before existing routes, leave /r//p//app/ routes and the fallback untouched.
Inlines CSS and JS rather than serving separate assets, typical of edge-function-served HTML.
Depends on env var SUPABASE_ANON_KEY via Deno.env.get with a '' fallback.
concerns: supabaseUrl is referenced but not defined in this file, so this snippet cannot compile standalone — correctness depends on the target module providing it.
BLOCK 1 (the route interception) exists only as comment text, so the actual routing wiring is not enforced by this file.
Loads the supabase-js client from an external CDN, creating a runtime dependency on cdn.jsdelivr.net availability.
notes: An additive OAuth-consent patch for the content-router edge function: paste-in route interceptor plus a serveConsent() that renders an inline email/phone-OTP login + approveAuthorization consent page for Vi.
questions: Where is supabaseUrl defined in the target content-router module?
Has BLOCK 1's route interception actually been applied to content-router, or does this patch remain unapplied?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file states it is the approved additive OAuth-consent patch, and its content is exactly that interceptor comment plus the serveConsent HTML function.
apparent_themes: Additive, non-destructive patching of an existing route handler
intention_signals: Aims to give Vi/Claude Design a hosted consent screen so a user can sign in (email or phone OTP) and approve an OAuth authorization for access to their design channel and submissions, wired into the existing content-router without disturbing its current routes.
novelty: The patch-as-instructions format (comments describing exactly where to insert each block) combined with a complete inline OAuth approval UI is distinctive.
contribution: Contributes a concrete OAuth consent screen and approveAuthorization integration plus a documented additive-insertion recipe for the content-router, usable as the authorization-grant surface of a first-party connector.
summary_for_embedding: A TypeScript additive patch for a Supabase content-router edge function that intercepts /oauth/consent and serves an inline HTML OAuth consent page with email/phone OTP sign-in and approveAuthorization for the Vi connector.
intention_for_embedding: To provide and wire in a first-party OAuth consent/authorization surface for Vi (Claude Design) so users can sign in and grant access to their Company design channel and submissions.
